name: Ngemelis Wall
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau, Rock Islands, Ngemelis
description: Ngemelis Wall drops dramatically into the abyss, starting at just 5 meters and plunging beyond 300 meters. The sheer wall is covered in vibrant soft corals, sea fans, and sponges, creating a dazzling display. Schools of barracuda, jacks, and reef sharks patrol the blue, while turtles and eagle rays glide along the reef. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, attracting pelagics and ensuring excellent visibility. The site offers thrilling drift dives with stunning vertical relief, making it a must for advanced divers.

name: Ryuko Maru
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau, Rock Islands, Meyungs
description: The wreck rests in clear Pacific waters near Palau's Rock Islands, surrounded by vibrant coral. A World War II Japanese freighter, it sits upright at 30 meters, encrusted with soft corals and home to schools of batfish, barracuda, and reef sharks. Penetration is possible through holds and the engine room, but currents can be strong. Ideal for advanced divers, the site offers excellent visibility and rich marine life. Nearby reefs add to the dive's appeal.

name: Urakami Maru
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau, Rock Islands, Ngeruktabel
description: This WWII wreck rests in shallow turquoise waters near the Rock Islands. The intact hull lies at 12-30 meters, encrusted with vibrant corals and swarming with reef fish. Schools of barracuda and jacks patrol above, while turtles and reef sharks frequent the site. Excellent visibility and calm conditions make it ideal for divers of all levels. The wreck's accessible depth allows long bottom times to explore its history and marine life. Nearby reefs add to the dive's diversity.

name: Yellow Beach Drop-Off
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau, Rock Islands, Peleliu
description: This dramatic wall dive in Palau's Rock Islands plunges into the Philippine Sea, offering stunning coral formations and abundant marine life. The steep drop-off starts at 5 meters, descending beyond 30 meters with vibrant soft corals and sea fans. Schools of barracuda, reef sharks, and occasional manta rays patrol the blue. Strong currents attract pelagics, making it ideal for drift diving. The shallow reef top provides excellent snorkeling with turtles and clownfish. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. Best dived on incoming tide when conditions are calmest.

name: Wild West Coral Garden
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau
description: This vibrant coral garden in Palau dazzles with towering pinnacles and dramatic drop-offs. Schools of batfish and barracuda patrol the blue, while reef sharks cruise the edges. Soft corals sway in mild currents, revealing hidden nudibranchs and colorful anthias. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, with warm water year-round. The site's steep walls and swim-throughs create exciting exploration for all levels. Night dives reveal octopus and hunting trevallies under the beams of your torch.

name: Turtle Reef
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau, Rock Islands, Ngeaur Island
description: This dive site near Ngeaur Island features a stunning coral reef teeming with green sea turtles. The sloping reef drops to 30 meters, with hard and soft corals creating a vibrant underwater landscape. Expect frequent turtle sightings, along with reef sharks, barracuda, and schools of tropical fish. Clear visibility and mild currents make it suitable for all skill levels. The site's healthy coral formations and abundant marine life showcase Palau's pristine diving conditions.

name: Lignite
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau
description: This dive site in Palau features dramatic underwater cliffs and caves, with visibility often exceeding 30 meters. Schools of barracuda and jacks patrol the blue, while reef sharks cruise the drop-offs. The site's volcanic rock formations create swim-throughs and overhangs teeming with colorful soft corals. Strong currents attract pelagic action, making it ideal for drift diving. Depth ranges from 10 to 40 meters, suitable for advanced divers. The warm waters host turtles, eagle rays, and occasional manta sightings.

name: Samidare
region: Oceania, Micronesia, Palau, Rock Islands, Kayangel
description: This dive site in Palau's Rock Islands features a steep wall plunging into the Philippine Sea. Vibrant soft corals and sea fans cover the drop-off, attracting reef sharks, barracuda, and schools of jacks. Strong currents bring pelagic action, making it ideal for drift diving. The top reef at 10 meters offers shallower exploration with turtles and colorful reef fish. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. The site sits near Kayangel Atoll, surrounded by the Pacific's deep blue waters.
